pr.title: fix(vscode): show Qwen 3.7 Max cache support
pr.body:
### Related Issue

**Issue:** #XXXX

### Description

Fixes Qwen3.7 Max prompt-cache reporting for the VSCode Cline provider path.

Cline's model endpoint exposes `qwen/qwen3.7-max` as `Qwen: Qwen3.7 Max` and includes cache-read pricing, but the extension still marked it as not prompt-cache capable because Cline model metadata only enabled prompt caching for hardcoded provider families. That made the model details/UI say prompt caching was unsupported even though the live Cline route returns cache reads.

This PR:

- marks Cline models as prompt-cache capable when Cline's model API returns cache read or write pricing
- adds `qwen/qwen3.7-max` to the OpenRouter-format cache-control model set used by the Cline provider request path
- keeps the selected/display model ID as `qwen/qwen3.7-max`; no Cline provider alias is used

The Cline backend currently reports Qwen3.7 Max cache reads, not cache writes, on this route. That matches the Cline model API pricing, which has `input_cache_read` but no `input_cache_write` for `qwen/qwen3.7-max`.

### Test Procedure

- Verified Cline model metadata for `qwen/qwen3.7-max` returns cache-read pricing (`input_cache_read: 0.00000025`) and no cache-write pricing.
- Ran live Cline provider probes for `qwen/qwen3.7-max`:
  - no `cache_control`: turn 1 `cachedTokens=0`, turn 2 `cachedTokens=10880`
  - with `cache_control`: turn 1 `cachedTokens=0`, turn 2 `cachedTokens=10880`
- Ran live VSCode `ClineHandler` path with selected model `qwen/qwen3.7-max`:
  - turn 1: `cacheWriteTokens=0`, `cacheReadTokens=0`, `inputTokens=11789`, `outputTokens=36`
  - turn 2: `cacheWriteTokens=0`, `cacheReadTokens=10880`, `inputTokens=909`, `outputTokens=50`
- Compared raw OpenRouter and Vercel Qwen3.7 Max routes; those providers report explicit cache writes and reads with `cache_control`, while the Cline route reports read hits only.
- Ran focused unit tests from `apps/vscode`:
  - `TS_NODE_PROJECT=./tsconfig.unit-test.json npx mocha --no-config --require ts-node/register --require source-map-support/register --require ./src/test/requires.ts src/core/controller/models/__tests__/refreshClineModels.test.ts src/core/api/transform/__tests__/openrouter-stream.test.ts src/core/api/providers/__tests__/cline.test.ts --exit`
  - result: `11 passing`
- Ran `npm run compile` from `apps/vscode`, including protobuf generation, type checks, lint, proto lint, and esbuild.

### Type of Change

-   [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
-   [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
-   [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
-   [ ] ♻️ Refactor Changes
-   [ ] 💅 Cosmetic Changes
-   [ ] 📚 Documentation update
-   [ ] 🏃 Workflow Changes

### Pre-flight Checklist

-   [x] Changes are limited to a single feature, bugfix or chore (split larger changes into separate PRs)
-   [x] Tests are passing (`npm test`) and code is formatted and linted (`npm run format && npm run lint`)
-   [x] I have reviewed [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/cline/cline/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Screenshots

N/A; request routing and usage accounting behavior only.

### Additional Notes

Cline provider Qwen3.7 Max caching is working as read-hit caching. The extension bug was that cache support was not shown from Cline metadata, and Qwen3.7 Max was missing from the cache-control marker list used by the Cline provider request transformer.
pr.author: arafatkatze
